# Last Time

::: {.incremental}
+ **Introduction to Arrays**
+ **Array Initialization**
+ **Indexing Array Elements**
+ **Accessing Array Elements in Loops**
+ **Inputting and Displaying Array Data**
+ **Vectors vs. Arrays**
:::

::: notes
Recall the fixed-size array model, valid index range, initialization, and traversal patterns used in the previous lecture.
:::

# Today's Agenda

- Arrays with Functions
- Array Algorithms
- Parallel Arrays

::: notes

:::

{{< include topic_1_arrays_functions.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_2_array_algorithms.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_3_parallel_arrays.qmd >}}

---

# Key Takeaways

- Built-in arrays decay at function boundaries, so functions receive an address and need a separate logical size
- Array algorithms combine traversal with accumulators, trackers, sentinels, or coordinated swaps
- Parallel arrays represent one record through a shared index that must remain synchronized

---

# Next Time

---

# Appendix

---

## Arrays with Functions: Good Practices

- Pass the logical size with every built-in array parameter
- Use `const` for read-only access
- Choose names that make mutation visible

---

## Arrays with Functions: Common Mistakes

- Assuming the function receives a copy of the array
- Expecting a bracketed parameter size to be enforced
- Using `sizeof` on a decayed array parameter

---

## Arrays with Functions: More Examples

```{.cpp}
int countMatches(const int values[], int size, int target) {
    int count = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < size; i++) {
        if (values[i] == target) {
            count++;
        }
    }
    return count;
}
```

---

## Arrays with Functions: Practice Problems

- Write `contains` to return whether a target appears in an array
- Write `clampToZero` to replace every negative element with zero
- State the correct parameter contract for each function

---

## Arrays with Functions: AI Search Terms

- C++ built-in array function parameter decay
- C++ const array parameter and size
- C++ sizeof array inside function

---

## Arrays with Functions: Questions for Reflection

- What information disappears when an array crosses a function boundary?
- How does `const` improve a function contract?
- When should a function receive one element instead of the whole array?

---

## Array Algorithms: Good Practices

- State empty-array behavior before implementing an algorithm
- Seed extrema from the first valid element
- Return an index when the caller needs an element's location

---

## Array Algorithms: Common Mistakes

- Initializing a maximum tracker to zero
- Reading index `size`
- Overwriting a value before preserving it during a swap

---

## Array Algorithms: More Examples

```{.cpp}
int countAbove(const int values[], int size, int threshold) {
    int count = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < size; i++) {
        if (values[i] > threshold) {
            count++;
        }
    }
    return count;
}
```

---

## Array Algorithms: Practice Problems

- Return the index of the smallest element
- Count how many elements fall within an inclusive range
- Trace two bubble-sort passes by hand

---

## Array Algorithms: AI Search Terms

- C++ array accumulator tracker sentinel patterns
- C++ linear search return index
- C++ bubble sort shrinking inner bound

---

## Array Algorithms: Questions for Reflection

- Why does a search usually return an index instead of a Boolean?
- Which algorithms require a nonempty array?
- What changes when an algorithm mutates its input?

---

## Parallel Arrays: Good Practices

- Use one capacity and one logical size
- Pass and traverse all related arrays together
- Return indexes when selecting complete records

---

## Parallel Arrays: Common Mistakes

- Moving one field without moving the others
- Looping to capacity instead of logical size
- Using different indexes for related fields

---

## Parallel Arrays: More Examples

```{.cpp}
void markPerfect(const int scores[], bool perfect[], int size) {
    for (int i = 0; i < size; i++) {
        perfect[i] = scores[i] == 100;
    }
}
```

---

## Parallel Arrays: Practice Problems

- Display only records whose attendance value is true
- Find a name and update the score at the matching index
- Sort names and scores together by descending score

---

## Parallel Arrays: AI Search Terms

- C++ parallel arrays shared index
- C++ sort parallel arrays together
- parallel arrays versus array of structs

---

## Parallel Arrays: Questions for Reflection

- What invariant connects the arrays?
- Why is an index more useful than a copied maximum value?
- When does a structure become the clearer design?
